# Compliance Inventory (auto-generated)

Generated by `scripts/compliance-inventory.py` on 2026-04-18.

This is the canonical manifest of every git-tracked file in NereusSDR,
classified by upstream lineage into one of ten buckets:

- `thetis-port` / `aethersdr-port` / `mi0bot-port` — files derived from the named upstream
- `wdsp-vendored` / `fftw3-vendored` — third-party code vendored verbatim under `third_party/`
- `nereussdr-original` — authored in-house, no upstream lineage
- `attribution-doc` / `docs` / `test` / `packaging` / `resource` — non-source classifications

Regenerate whenever `THETIS-PROVENANCE.md`, `aethersdr-reconciliation.md`,
`WDSP-PROVENANCE.md`, or the source tree changes. Drift between this
file and the current tree is a CI failure (see Task 15 of
`docs/architecture/2026-04-18-gpl-compliance-full-audit-plan.md`).

The ⚠ flag on an entry means the file's expected license-header markers
were not found in its head. Zero flags today means the tree passes the
baseline verifier; new flags on regeneration indicate regression.

See `docs/attribution/README.md` for the relationship between this
inventory and per-component provenance docs.

---
